question.text: What is the maximum buyback limit as a percentage of aggregate paid-up capital and free reserves under SEBI Buyback Regulations?

answer.text: 10% of aggregate paid-up capital and free reserves
25% of aggregate paid-up capital and free reserves
50% of aggregate paid-up capital and free reserves
15% of aggregate paid-up capital and free reserves

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI Buyback of Securities Regulations, 2018, a company can buy back up to 25% of the aggregate of paid-up capital and free reserves, based on standalone or consolidated financials, whichever is LOWER. **The Trap:** 10% is the threshold for board-approved buyback under Companies Act (without shareholder approval), which candidates often confuse with the SEBI maximum limit. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) and NISM Series XV (Research Analyst) also test buyback basics as corporate actions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always check both standalone and consolidated figures — the lower one governs the maximum buyback size, catching many companies off guard.
